items.claim: Cody Gibson is a tall lanky guy for the bantamweight division and uses his length well with a good jab and teeps.
items.quote: He's a good striker, uses his length really well. He's a tall lanky guy for the division and he uses it as such. Good jab, good teeps

items.claim: Cody Gibson gets backed up to the cage, which takes the sting off his shots and allows opponents to negate his range.
items.quote: the problem is he does get backed up and that can cost him a lot because when you're getting backed up and you're this tall lanky fellow, it takes a lot of the sting off your shots and also it gets them to back you up to the cage where they can negate some of that range.

items.claim: Cody Gibson is a good grappler with solid wrestling fundamentals and is very active from his back with scrambles and elbows.
items.quote: He's a good grappler, too. He's got really solid wrestling fundamentals. He's a good back taker and even if he's on his back, he's very active from his back. Scrambles really well, nasty elbows in the ground and pound.

items.claim: Abdul Hussein has a solid choke arsenal and can snatch up various chokes from anywhere.
items.quote: He's got a solid choke arsenal. He can snatch up various chokes from pretty much anywhere.

items.claim: Mohammad Said can slow down, especially if he is forced to grapple.
items.quote: Now, he can slow down. That can be a problem for him, especially if he's forced to grapple.

items.claim: Dustin Jacoby has surprising power and a ton of knockouts from accumulation and heavy hits rather than one-shot freak power.
items.quote: surprising power. People always forget that Dustin Jacoby's powerful because he doesn't look like a power puncher, but the dude has a ton of knockouts.

items.claim: Dustin Jacoby has good takedown defense and immediately transitions to striking after stuffing a takedown.
items.quote: He's also got good takedown defense and as soon as he stuffs a takedown, he goes right into striking which makes it really hard for people who want to continually shoot takedowns.

items.claim: Sam Patterson has tall man defense where he keeps his chin up to be hit.
items.quote: he does have that tall man defense. And if Sam Patterson loses this fight that's probably why. Because he does just kind of have his chin up there to be hit.

items.claim: Sam Patterson has good cardio despite being a bigger fighter for his division.
items.quote: He's also got pretty good cardio. You don't usually think of a guy that is kind of like bigger for the division to have good cardio.

items.claim: Santiago Ponzinibbio never quite came back the same after suffering a big injury a few years ago.
items.quote: ever since Ponzi had that big injury like few years ago, more than a few years ago, quite a while ago, he never quite came back the same.

items.claim: Mike Davis is a good striker with good foot pressure, combinations, level changes, and knees after boxing combos.
items.quote: He's a good striker with good foot pressure, really good combinations. I love the combinations. He'll change his levels in combination... And I love the knees after the boxing combo.

items.claim: Mike Davis has traditionally good takedown defense in a sprawl.
items.quote: traditionally he's got pretty good takedowns or takedown defense, sorry.

items.claim: Nurullo Aliev's striking is set up by his grappling threat and pressure against the cage and on the mat.
items.quote: It's basically the grappling threat sets it up and it sets up his flurries... because his grappling is such a threat, because he will smother you on the mat, he can start landing on the feet.
